{ "goal": "Implement 6 UI/UX research-backed decisions from docs/ui-ux-decisions.md: (1) Semantic colors for status components with WCAG AA-validated palette, (2) Progressive disclosure on My Week page, (3) AI suggestion tiering to reduce fatigue, (4) Dual font stack (Inter + Geist Mono) via host-token pipeline, (5) Charts library migration to Tremor/Recharts, (6) Loading & empty state improvements. Changes are scoped to the frontend package and the shared DesignTokens contract.", "steps": [ { "id": "step-1", "title": "Add WCAG AA-validated semantic color tokens to CSS theme", "description": "Decision 1, Part 1: Define semantic color tokens in `frontend/src/index.css`. The gap analysis found that several proposed palette values fail WCAG AA contrast for normal text (4.5:1 minimum). Before implementing, use corrected values that pass contrast checks: **Light mode (on white-ish backgrounds):** - Success base: `#3D7A4A` (was `#5F7A66`, boosted saturation/darkness for ≥4.5:1 on `#EEF3EF`) - Warning base: `#7A6520` (was `#8A7A5A`, darkened for ≥4.5:1 on `#F5F2EA`) - Danger base: `#9B3B3B` (was `#8A5C5C`, increased contrast for ≥4.5:1 on `#F6EEEE`) - Info/AI base: `#4A5578` (was `#5F6B85`, darkened for ≥4.5:1 on `#EEF1F6`) **Dark mode (on dark backgrounds):** - Success base: `#8FBF9A` (was `#7E9B87`, lightened for ≥4.5:1 on `#1A221C`) - Warning base: `#C4AC6A` (was `#A39473`, lightened for ≥4.5:1 on `#211F18`) - Danger base: `#D4A0A0` (was `#A37878`, lightened for ≥4.5:1 on `#221A1A`) - Info/AI base: `#9DABC8` (was `#7C8AA6`, lightened for ≥4.5:1 on `#1A1D24`) Background and border values from the original plan are fine (they don't carry text). Add new CSS custom properties in the `@theme` block: - `--color-success`, `--color-success-bg`, `--color-success-border` - `--color-warning`, `--color-warning-bg`, `--color-warning-border` - `--color-danger`, `--color-danger-bg`, `--color-danger-border` - `--color-info`, `--color-info-bg`, `--color-info-border` Update the existing `--color-success`, `--color-warning`, `--color-danger` values (which currently are gray `#404040`/`#737373`/`#171717` in light, vivid in dark) to the new muted semantic values. Add new `-bg` and `-border` tokens. Also update the `[data-theme="dark"]` block accordingly. Keep `--color-success-foreground`, `--color-warning-foreground`, `--color-danger-foreground` valid for button use (ensure they contrast against the base colors). Update `MockHostProvider.tsx` `defaultDesignTokens` to match the new light-mode success/warning/danger values. Update `AppShell.tsx` `tokensToStyle` to pass through the new token values. Note: The `DesignTokens` interface in `packages/shared/src/host.ts` already has `colorSuccess`, `colorWarning`, `colorDanger` — these continue to work. The new `-bg` and `-border` tokens are CSS-only (not part of the host contract) since they are implementation details of the module's internal design system.", "requiredChecks": ["lint", "typecheck", "build"] }, { "id": "step-2", "title": "Update Badge, Toast, and Button components for semantic colors", "description": "Decision 1, Part 2: Update status components to use the new semantic color tokens instead of monochrome foreground-based styling. **Badge.tsx** — Replace the `success`, `warning`, `danger` variant classes: - `success`: `border-success-border bg-success-bg text-success` (was `border-foreground/25 bg-foreground/5 text-foreground`) - `warning`: `border-warning-border bg-warning-bg text-warning` (was `border-foreground/15 bg-foreground/[0.03] text-muted`) - `danger`: `border-danger-border bg-danger-bg text-danger font-bold` (was `border-foreground/30 bg-foreground/8 text-foreground font-bold`) - Add new `info` variant: `border-info-border bg-info-bg text-info` Keep plan-state badge variants (`draft`, `locked`, `reconciling`, `reconciled`) monochrome. **Toast.tsx** — Update `toastVariants` to use semantic tokens: - `success`: `border-success-border bg-success-bg text-success` - `warning`: `border-warning-border bg-warning-bg text-warning` - `error`: `border-danger-border bg-danger-bg text-danger` - `info`: `border-info-border bg-info-bg text-info` **Button.tsx** — Update `danger` and `success` button variants to use the new semantic base colors. The `danger` variant: `bg-danger text-white` (ensure white contrasts against the new danger base). The `success` variant: `bg-success text-white`. Register the new color tokens in `@theme` so Tailwind utilities like `text-success`, `bg-success-bg`, `border-success-border` etc. resolve properly. Update `frontend/src/__tests__/ui/Badge.test.tsx` assertions — tests currently check for `text-foreground` and `text-muted` on semantic variants; update to check for `text-success`, `text-warning`, `text-danger` respectively.", "requiredChecks": ["lint", "typecheck", "unit", "build"] }, { "id": "step-3", "title": "Apply semantic colors to status indicators across the app", "description": "Decision 1, Part 3: Update all status-indicating components to use semantic colors with icon + text label redundancy. **MyWeek.tsx — ComplianceBadge**: Already uses `text-success`/`text-warning` — will now resolve to the new muted semantic colors automatically. Verify the ✓/⚠ icon + text pattern is preserved. **MyWeek.tsx — PlanStateBadge**: Keep monochrome (plan states are not severity indicators). **Reports.tsx — ExceptionAgingTable**: Update `SEVERITY_STYLES` map: - `CRITICAL`: use danger semantic tokens - `HIGH`: use warning semantic tokens - `MEDIUM`: keep neutral - `LOW`: keep neutral Update the STALE/AGING/NEW status labels to use `text-danger`/`text-warning`/`text-muted` (already correct). **Reports.tsx — VelocityTrendChart**: Trend indicator already uses `text-success`/`text-danger`. **Reports.tsx — ComplianceChart**: Update Shield icon to use `text-success` (already correct). **InsightPanel.tsx — severityBadgeVariant**: Already maps HIGH→danger, MEDIUM→warning — now those Badge variants will render with semantic colors. **ProactiveRiskBanner.tsx**: Update `SIGNAL_CONFIG` classes from `border-neutral-400 bg-neutral-100` to use danger/warning semantic background tokens for severity differentiation. **RiskSignalsPanel.tsx**: Update `SIGNAL_CLS` from neutral grays to semantic tokens based on signal severity. **ScopeChangeDialog.tsx**: Already uses `text-success`/`text-danger` for action type labels. **ReconcileAssistPanel.tsx**: Update `OUTCOME_COLORS` to use semantic tokens for ACHIEVED (success), NOT_ACHIEVED (danger), PARTIALLY_ACHIEVED (warning). **TicketDetailView.tsx**: Already maps outcomes to `text-success`/`text-warning`/`text-danger`. Ensure every status color use has icon + text label for accessibility redundancy (not color alone).", "requiredChecks": ["lint", "typecheck", "build"] }, { "id": "step-4", "title": "Install Inter font and update font token pipeline for dual font stack", "description": "Decision 4, Part 1: Switch the base font from monospace to proportional sans-serif. The font pipeline flows through 3 layers: (1) `packages/shared/src/host.ts` DesignTokens contract, (2) `frontend/src/host/MockHostProvider.tsx` default values, (3) `frontend/src/components/AppShell.tsx` which sets `--font-family-base` as an inline CSS var, and (4) `frontend/src/index.css` which uses it in `@theme` and `body`. **Changes:** 1. **`frontend/src/index.css`**: Add Google Fonts import for Inter alongside Geist Mono: `@import url("https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@300..900&family=Geist+Mono:wght@300..900&display=swap");` Update `@theme` `--font-family-base` default to: `var(--font-family-base, "Inter", "Geist Sans", system-ui, sans-serif)` Keep `--font-family-mono` as-is. 2. **`frontend/src/host/MockHostProvider.tsx`**: Update `defaultDesignTokens.fontFamilyBase` from the monospace string to `"'Inter', 'Geist Sans', system-ui, sans-serif"`. 3. **`frontend/src/components/AppShell.tsx`**: No structural changes needed — `tokensToStyle` already passes `tokens.fontFamilyBase` through to `--font-family-base`. The inline `fontFamily: 'var(--font-family-base)'` on the root div continues to work. 4. **Note on `packages/shared/src/host.ts`**: The `DesignTokens.fontFamilyBase` field is a string — no type change needed. The contract is that the host provides a font family string; we're just changing the default. This step only changes the default font. The next step handles adding `font-mono` classes to preserve monospace where needed.", "requiredChecks": ["lint", "typecheck", "build"] }, { "id": "step-5", "title": "Add font-mono utility classes to code-like content", "description": "Decision 4, Part 2: Now that the base font is proportional, explicitly apply `font-mono` (which maps to `--font-family-mono` = Geist Mono) to code-like content that should remain monospace. Audit and update these components: **Already using `font-mono`** (no change needed): - `TicketListView.tsx` — ticket keys - `TicketDetailView.tsx` — ticket keys - `UncommittedWorkSection.tsx` — ticket keys - `WhatIfPanel.tsx` — IDs **Need `font-mono` added:** - `CommitList.tsx` — commit titles (add `font-mono` to the title text span) - `CommitForm.tsx` — the title input field (add `font-mono` class) - `CapacityMeter.tsx` — estimate point values (the tally `{totalPoints} / {budgetPoints} pts`) - `Reports.tsx` — data cells in charts (point values, percentages, date labels marked as `text-[9px]` or `text-[10px]`) - `ScopeChangeTimeline.tsx` — commit titles in timeline events - `RcdoTreeView.tsx` — RCDO path identifiers - `AiLintPanel.tsx` — no change (prose suggestions should be sans-serif) - `Header.tsx` — week date display (date/time values) **Should NOT get font-mono** (keep proportional): - Navigation labels - Page headings (h1, h2) - Card titles - Button text - Body text / descriptions - Form labels - AI insight text - AI summary prose Update related tests if any assert on class names that would change.", "requiredChecks": ["lint", "typecheck", "unit", "build"] }, { "id": "step-6", "title": "Create CollapsibleSection shared component with localStorage persistence", "description": "Decision 2, Prerequisite: Build a reusable `CollapsibleSection` component before refactoring My Week. Create `frontend/src/components/shared/CollapsibleSection.tsx`: ```tsx interface CollapsibleSectionProps { id: string; // localStorage key suffix title: string; // Section heading defaultExpanded?: boolean; badge?: ReactNode; // e.g., "3 suggestions" count badge children: ReactNode; } ``` Features: - Renders a header row with title, optional badge, and chevron toggle - Persists expand/collapse state to `localStorage` under key `wc-section-{id}` - Supports `defaultExpanded` prop (used on first visit before localStorage has a value) - Accessible: uses `aria-expanded`, `aria-controls`, and `role="region"` on content - Styled consistently with the existing Card/CardHeader pattern (border-border, bg-surface) - Smooth height transition using CSS `grid-template-rows` trick (0fr → 1fr) Also create `frontend/src/components/shared/usePersistedState.ts` — a generic hook for localStorage-backed state used by CollapsibleSection and potentially other components. Write unit tests in `frontend/src/__tests__/CollapsibleSection.test.tsx`: - Renders title and children - Toggles open/close on click - Respects defaultExpanded prop - Persists state to localStorage - Renders badge when provided", "requiredChecks": ["lint", "typecheck", "unit", "build"] }, { "id": "step-7", "title": "Implement progressive disclosure on My Week page", "description": "Decision 2: Refactor `frontend/src/routes/MyWeek.tsx` to wrap sections in CollapsibleSection and add conditional visibility. **Always visible** (no change): - Plan header with state badge + action buttons - Capacity meter - Commit list - Week selector + page header **Contextually visible** (add conditional guards — some already exist): - Risk banners: already guarded by `isLocked` ✓ - Auto-lock system banner: already guarded by `plan?.systemLockedWithErrors` ✓ - Scope change timeline: already guarded by `isLocked && scopeChangeEvents.length > 0` ✓ - Pre-lock validation: already guarded by `isDraft && showPreLockValidation` ✓ **Wrap in CollapsibleSection** (with defaults): - AI Lint Panel: `...}>`. Extract lint result count to show as badge summary (e.g., "3 hints"). - AI Insights (InsightPanel): `` - What-If Planner: Already collapsed via its own UI ✓ — but wrap in CollapsibleSection for consistency - Plan History: Already has show/hide toggle — replace with CollapsibleSection for consistency - Carry-forward Lineage: Keep as-is (it's triggered by specific commit action, not always present) - Soft Warnings Panel: `` where `shouldExpandWarnings` is true when commits > 8 or pawn ratio > 40% **Add "Expand all / Collapse all" toggle** at the top of the page for power users. Implement as a button that programmatically sets all section localStorage keys. Preserve all existing `data-testid` attributes and ensure existing tests still pass.", "requiredChecks": ["lint", "typecheck", "unit", "build"] }, { "id": "step-8", "title": "Implement AI suggestion tiering and dismiss memory", "description": "Decision 3: Adjust AI component default states and add dismiss memory. **Changes to AI components:** 1. **AiLintPanel** (already addressed in step-7 via CollapsibleSection with collapsed default + badge summary). No additional changes needed beyond the collapse wrapper. 2. **InsightPanel** on My Week: Already addressed in step-7 (collapsed by default with CollapsibleSection). 3. **ManagerAiSummaryCard** on Team Week (`frontend/src/routes/TeamWeek.tsx`): Wrap in CollapsibleSection with `id="manager-ai-summary"` and `defaultExpanded={false}`. Show the first line of `summaryText` as a preview in the section header badge. 4. **ProactiveRiskBanner** on My Week: Add a count badge approach — show a summary line "⚠ N risk signals detected" that expands to full banners on click. Wrap in CollapsibleSection with `id="risk-banners"` and `defaultExpanded={true}` (critical signals should default open but can be collapsed). 5. **ReconcileAssistPanel** on Reconcile page: Change auto-fill behavior. Currently `onAcceptOutcome` applies immediately. Add a ghost/preview visual state — show suggested outcomes as faded text with a distinct `bg-info-bg` background and an "AI Suggested" badge. User clicks to solidify/accept. Use the existing `AiSuggestedBadge` component more prominently. 6. **TeamWeek InsightPanel**: Already has a toggle (`insightsExpanded`). Change default to collapsed: `const [insightsExpanded, setInsightsExpanded] = useState(false);` **Dismiss memory system:** Create `frontend/src/lib/useDismissMemory.ts` hook: - Tracks dismiss count per component ID in localStorage - `useDismissMemory(componentId: string, threshold?: number)` returns `{ shouldAutoCollapse: boolean, recordDismiss: () => void }` - Default threshold: 3 consecutive dismissals → auto-collapse - Wire into AiLintPanel and InsightPanel CollapsibleSection defaults **Global AI preference:** Add to the existing feature flags or create a new localStorage-based setting `wc-ai-mode` with values `'full'` | `'on-demand'`. When `'on-demand'`, all AI sections default to collapsed and auto-run is disabled. Add a small toggle in the sidebar footer or header settings area.", "requiredChecks": ["lint", "typecheck", "unit", "build"] }, { "id": "step-9", "title": "Create skeleton loading variants for major page surfaces", "description": "Decision 6, Part 1: Replace text-only loading states with skeleton loaders. The existing `Skeleton` component in `frontend/src/components/ui/Skeleton.tsx` is a simple animated div. Build composed skeleton variants: **Create `frontend/src/components/shared/skeletons/`:** 1. **`PlanHeaderSkeleton.tsx`**: Mimics the plan header Card layout — badge placeholder, action button placeholders. 2. **`CommitListSkeleton.tsx`**: Shows 3-4 skeleton commit rows matching the `CommitList` SortableItem layout — grip handle, title bar, chess piece dot, points, action buttons. 3. **`TeamWeekSkeleton.tsx`**: Overview card grid with skeleton stat boxes matching `OverviewSection`. 4. **`ReportChartSkeleton.tsx`**: Generic chart placeholder — card with header skeleton + rectangular area for chart body. **Update routes to use skeletons:** - `MyWeek.tsx`: Replace `
Loading plan…
` with `` + `` - `TeamWeek.tsx`: Replace `
Loading team data…
` with `` - `Reports.tsx`: Replace `

Loading report data…

` with grid of `` - `Rcdos.tsx`: Replace `Loading…` text with a tree-like skeleton - `Tickets.tsx`: Replace loading state with table skeleton rows **Tier 2 — AI loading states** (already partially implemented): - `InsightPanel` already has `InsightSkeletons` ✓ - `ManagerAiSummaryCard` already has `ManagerAiSummarySkeleton` ✓ - `AiLintPanel`: Update "Analyzing commit quality…" to include shimmer animation **Tier 3 — Timeout fallback:** Create `frontend/src/components/shared/LoadingWithTimeout.tsx`: - Shows skeleton for first N seconds - After timeout (default 5s), shows "Still loading… [Retry]" with elapsed time - Takes `onRetry` callback prop", "requiredChecks": ["lint", "typecheck", "unit", "build"] }, { "id": "step-10", "title": "Add empty state components for major surfaces", "description": "Decision 6, Part 2: Create rich empty states with description + CTA for each major surface. Create `frontend/src/components/shared/EmptyState.tsx`: ```tsx interface EmptyStateProps { icon?: ReactNode; // Lucide icon or emoji illustration title: string; // e.g., "No commits yet" description?: string; // Explanatory text action?: ReactNode; // CTA button 'data-testid'?: string; } ``` Styled consistently: centered content, muted text, subtle border, generous padding. **Apply to surfaces:** 1. **MyWeek (no plan / empty commits)**: `planLoading` is false and `!plan` → show EmptyState with title "Start your week", description "Add your first commitment to begin planning", CTA → Add Commit button. 2. **CommitList (empty)**: When `commits.length === 0` and plan exists → EmptyState "No commits yet" with prominent Add button. Currently shows nothing. 3. **TeamWeek (no team selected)**: Already has a "No team selected" div — enhance with EmptyState component for consistency. 4. **Reports (no data)**: When all report arrays are empty → EmptyState "Not enough data yet — reports will populate after your first reconciled week". 5. **RCDO tree (empty)**: When `tree` is empty array → EmptyState "No strategy nodes — create your first Rally Cry to get started" with CTA if user has permissions. 6. **Tickets (empty results)**: When ticket list returns 0 results → EmptyState "No tickets match your filters" or "No tickets yet — create your first ticket". Write unit test for EmptyState component.", "requiredChecks": ["lint", "typecheck", "unit", "build"] }, { "id": "step-11", "title": "Install Tremor charting library and migrate velocity trend chart", "description": "Decision 5, Part 1: Add Tremor as a dependency and migrate the first (most complex) chart as proof of concept. **Install:** `npm install @tremor/react` in the frontend package. Tremor is Tailwind-native and works with Tailwind v4. **Migrate `VelocityTrendChart`** in `frontend/src/routes/Reports.tsx`: - Replace the manual CSS bar chart + rolling average dots with Tremor's `AreaChart` or `BarChart` component - Include the rolling 4-week average as a second series (line overlay) - Preserve the trend indicator badge in the header - Add proper tooltips (Tremor provides them out of the box) - Ensure dark mode works (Tremor respects Tailwind dark mode) - Keep the same Card wrapper and header layout **Keep pure CSS for:** - `CapacityMeter` progress bar (simple, works well) - Chess distribution stacked bar (unique visual, Tremor doesn't have a great match) Verify bundle size impact is acceptable. If Tremor has issues with Tailwind v4 compatibility, fall back to Recharts (`npm install recharts`) which has no CSS framework dependency. Update any snapshot or assertion tests for Reports if they exist.", "requiredChecks": ["lint", "typecheck", "build"] }, { "id": "step-12", "title": "Migrate remaining charts to charting library", "description": "Decision 5, Part 2: Migrate the remaining CSS charts to the charting library chosen in step-11. **Charts to migrate:** 1. **PlannedVsAchievedChart**: Grouped bar chart (planned vs achieved per week). Use `BarChart` with two series. 2. **AchievementRateChart**: Bar chart with target line at 80%. Use `BarChart` with reference line. 3. **CarryForwardChart**: Horizontal bar chart showing carry-forward rates. Use `BarChart` horizontal or `BarList`. 4. **ComplianceChart**: Multi-series horizontal bars (lock rate, reconcile rate, auto-lock). Use grouped `BarChart`. 5. **ScopeChangeChart**: Vertical bars with HIGH annotation. Use `BarChart` with conditional coloring. **Keep as CSS:** - Chess distribution stacked bar (custom visual) - Exception aging table (it's a table, not a chart) - AI acceptance card bars (simple progress bars) Each migrated chart should: - Maintain the same Card wrapper and title - Use consistent color tokens (integrate with our semantic color system where appropriate) - Include proper ARIA labels and screen reader support - Handle empty data states gracefully - Support dark mode This is an incremental migration — each chart is a self-contained function component in Reports.tsx.", "requiredChecks": ["lint", "typecheck", "build"] } ] }

content.text:
# UI/UX Decision Guide — Research-Backed Recommendations

Generated from ChatGPT Pro/Deep Research + Claude Research analysis of the Weekly Commit Module frontend, cross-referenced against enterprise tool patterns (Linear, Notion, GitHub, Jira, Monday.com).

---

## Decision 1: Semantic Colors in the Monochrome System

### Problem
The current light-mode palette uses gray shades for success (#404040), warning (#737373), and danger (#171717). This makes it nearly impossible to quickly scan compliance status, risk signals, and plan states.

### Research Findings
- **Linear** (known for minimal design) uses semantic colored dots for issue status — **not monochrome**. The chrome recedes, but status colors are always present.
- **GitHub** uses subtle colored badges + strong red only for errors.
- **Best practice**: "Low-chroma by default, high-chroma on change" — neutral gray for steady state, muted semantic tones for status.
- **WCAG implication**: Gray-on-gray status indicators fail contrast requirements and cannot be distinguished by colorblind users.

### Recommendation: Add Muted Semantic Colors (Light Mode)
Keep the monochrome base but introduce **desaturated, restrained semantic colors** for status components only:

| Token | Light Mode | Dark Mode | Usage |
|-------|-----------|-----------|-------|
| **Success base** | `#5F7A66` | `#7E9B87` | Text/icons for compliant, achieved |
| **Success bg** | `#EEF3EF` | `#1A221C` | Badges, row highlights |
| **Success border** | `#C7D6CB` | `#2F3E33` | Card borders, left indicators |
| **Warning base** | `#8A7A5A` | `#A39473` | At-risk, over-budget |
| **Warning bg** | `#F5F2EA` | `#211F18` | Alert backgrounds |
| **Warning border** | `#DDD2B8` | `#3A3526` | Border accents |
| **Danger base** | `#8A5C5C` | `#A37878` | Missed deadline, critical |
| **Danger bg** | `#F6EEEE` | `#221A1A` | Error backgrounds |
| **Danger border** | `#E2CACA` | `#3B2A2A` | Border accents |
| **Info/AI base** | `#5F6B85` | `#7C8AA6` | AI suggestions, info |
| **Info/AI bg** | `#EEF1F6` | `#1A1D24` | AI panel backgrounds |
| **Info/AI border** | `#CCD3E0` | `#2F3542` | AI component borders |

**Always pair color with icon + text label** for redundancy (e.g., ✓ Compliant + green, ⚠ At Risk + amber).

**Scope of change**: Only status components — `Badge`, `ComplianceBadge`, `PlanStateBadge`, risk banners, exception severity indicators. Keep all chrome, navigation, and card backgrounds monochrome.

---

## Decision 2: Progressive Disclosure on My Week Page

### Problem
My Week renders 12+ sections simultaneously: plan header, risk banners, AI insights, lint panel, capacity meter, soft warnings, commit list, what-if planner, scope change timeline, AI composer, plan history, carry-forward lineage.

### Research Findings
- **Best practice**: Use a **hierarchical combination** — tabs for top-level, collapsible sections within, separate pages for deep workflows.
- **Linear** uses "role × recency × anomaly" to set smart defaults — expand what needs action, collapse the rest.
- **Key heuristic**: If users need to compare → tabs. If users need to scan → collapsibles. If users need to focus → new page.

### Recommendation: Smart Defaults + Collapsible Sections

**Always visible** (core workflow):
- Plan header with state badge + action buttons
- Capacity meter
- Commit list (the primary interaction surface)

**Contextually visible** (appear only when relevant):
- Risk banners → only when plan is LOCKED and risks exist
- Auto-lock system banner → only when system-locked
- Scope change timeline → only when post-lock changes exist
- Pre-lock validation → only when user clicks Lock

**Default collapsed** (available on demand):
- AI Lint Panel → collapsed with summary badge "3 suggestions" — expand to see details
- AI Insights → collapsed with one-line preview
- What-If Planner → already collapsed (good ✓)
- Plan History → already collapsed (good ✓)
- Carry-forward Lineage → already collapsed (good ✓)
- Soft Warnings → collapsed unless critical (>8 commits or >40% pawns)

**Persist expansion state** to localStorage so returning users see their preferred layout.

**Add "Expand all / Collapse all"** toggle for power users.

---

## Decision 3: AI Suggestion UX Patterns

### Problem
The app has 10+ AI touchpoints. Risk of "AI fatigue" — users learn to ignore suggestions.

### Research Findings
- **Gold standard (2024-2025)**: Ghost text / inline suggestions that require **affirmative action to accept** (opt-in), not opt-out to reject.
- **Graduated engagement model**:
  - Level 1 (Passive): Subtle inline hints, ghost text
  - Level 2 (Contextual): Appear only after user action (on save, on empty state)
  - Level 3 (Explicit): User clicks "Generate" or "Suggest"
- **Fatigue prevention**: Respect dismissal memory, confidence gating, rate limiting per session, per-component "turn off" option.
- **AI pre-fill best practice**: Visual distinction (different background, "AI Suggested" badge), but content is editable inline — NOT a separate accept/reject modal for each field.

### Recommendation: Tiered AI Surface

| AI Capability | Current Pattern | Recommended Pattern |
|---|---|---|
| **Commit Lint** | Auto-runs, shows panel | ✅ Good — make it a **collapsed summary badge** ("3 hints") that expands on click |
| **AI Commit Composer** | Modal dialog | ✅ Good — explicit invocation via button |
| **RCDO Suggestion** | Inline during editing | ✅ Good — keep as inline ghost suggestion |
| **Reconcile pre-fill** | Auto-fills outcomes | ⚠️ Change to **ghost/preview state** — show suggested outcomes as faded text that solidifies on click, not pre-selected |
| **Risk Banners** | Always visible when locked | ⚠️ Show **count badge** first, expand to details on click |
| **AI Insights** | Auto-expanded panel | ⚠️ Collapse by default with one-line summary |
| **Manager Summary** | Auto-expanded card | ⚠️ Show summary header, expand details on click |
| **Semantic Search** | Input always visible | ✅ Good — explicit invocation |
| **What-If** | Collapsed by default | ✅ Good |
| **Draft Assist** | On-demand button | ✅ Good |

**Add per-component dismiss memory**: If a user dismisses lint suggestions 3x in a row, collapse by default for that user.

**Add global AI preference**: Settings toggle to reduce AI suggestions to "on-demand only" mode.

---

## Decision 4: Typography — Monospace as Primary Font

### Problem
Geist Mono is used as the **only** font for all text (headings, body, labels, navigation, charts). Users spend 10-15 min/session.

### Research Findings
- **Monospace is NOT recommended as primary UI font** for form-heavy enterprise tools.
- Downsides: lower readability for dense text, poor visual hierarchy (uniform character width), feels overly technical.
- **Linear uses Inter** (proportional sans-serif) as its primary font.
- **Best practice**: Sans-serif for UI chrome (labels, navigation, body text), monospace reserved for code, IDs, tabular numeric data.
- **Exception**: Developer-first tools (terminals, observability) can lean heavier on monospace, but still keep UI chrome in sans-serif.

### Recommendation: Dual Font Stack

```css
--font-family-base: "Inter", "Geist Sans", system-ui, sans-serif;
--font-family-mono: "Geist Mono", ui-monospace, monospace;
```

**Use proportional (Inter/Geist Sans) for**:
- Navigation labels
- Page headings
- Card titles
- Button text
- Body text / descriptions
- Form labels

**Keep monospace (Geist Mono) for**:
- Commit titles (they're "code-like" work contracts)
- Ticket IDs
- RCDO paths
- Estimate points
- Date/time values
- Report data cells
- Code-like identifiers

This preserves the "utilitarian/technical" feel while dramatically improving readability for the 70%+ of text that's prose.

---

## Decision 5: Charts — CSS vs Charting Library

### Problem
Reports page renders 8 chart types (velocity trend with rolling average, planned vs achieved, achievement rate, chess distribution, scope changes, carry-forward, compliance, exception aging) using pure CSS divs. No charting library.

### Research Findings
- Pure CSS charts are viable for ≤ 2-3 simple chart types with minimal interaction.
- **Switch threshold**: ≥ 4-5 chart types, any time-series, multi-series, or interactive charts.
- The project has **8 types** — already past the threshold.
- Key gaps in current CSS approach: manual accessibility (ARIA roles, screen reader support), fragile tooltips, no coordinated animation, manual responsive scaling, duplicated axis/stacking logic.

### Recommendation: Adopt Recharts or Tremor

| Library | Best For | Bundle | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Tremor** | Fastest for business dashboards | ~50KB | Opinionated, Tailwind-native, perfect for this stack |
| **Recharts** | Most flexibility | ~80KB | Most popular React chart lib, good docs |
| **Nivo** | Complex visualizations | ~100KB+ | Overkill for this use case |

**Recommended: Tremor** — it's Tailwind-native, designed for exactly this kind of enterprise dashboard, and ships with accessible, responsive charts out of the box.

**Keep pure CSS for**: simple progress bars (capacity meter), inline sparklines, the stacked chess distribution bar.

**Migrate to library for**: velocity trend, planned vs achieved, achievement rate, carry-forward, compliance, scope changes.

---

## Decision 6: Loading & Empty States

### Problem
Most pages show "Loading…" text. Skeleton component exists but is barely used. Empty states are minimal text.

### Research Findings
- Skeleton loaders are **superior for perceived performance** when layout is predictable and load time is 0.5-2s.
- Add timeout threshold (~2-3s): switch from skeleton → explicit progress state ("Still loading…", retry option).
- Empty states should include **illustration + description + CTA** (e.g., "No commits yet — add your first commit to start planning your week").

### Recommendation: Three-Tier Loading Strategy

**Tier 1 — Skeleton (< 2s expected)**:
- Plan header
- Commit list (show 3-4 skeleton commit rows)
- Team Week overview cards
- Report chart placeholders

**Tier 2 — Progress indicator (2-5s expected)**:
- AI suggestions (show shimmer + "AI is analyzing…")
- Semantic search results

**Tier 3 — Explicit state (> 5s or uncertain)**:
- "Still loading… [Retry]" with elapsed time
- AI unavailable fallback

**Empty states** — add for each major surface:
- My Week (no plan): illustration + "Start your week — add your first commitment"
- Commit list (empty): "No commits yet" + prominent Add button
- Team Week (no team): clear guidance on team selection
- Reports (no data): "Not enough data yet — reports will populate after your first reconciled week"
- RCDO tree (empty): "No strategy nodes — create your first Rally Cry to get started"

---

## Priority Order for Implementation

1. **Semantic colors** (highest impact, lowest effort) — swap 6 CSS vars
2. **Typography dual font stack** (high readability impact) — swap font vars, add font-mono utility classes
3. **Progressive disclosure on My Week** (reduce overwhelm) — wrap sections in collapsible containers
4. **Loading/empty states** (perceived quality) — add skeleton variants
5. **AI suggestion tiering** (reduce fatigue) — adjust default collapsed states
6. **Charts library migration** (medium effort, polish) — incremental per-chart migration

---

## Sources
- ChatGPT Pro extended analysis (March 2025)
- Claude Research with web search (March 2025)
- Linear design system analysis
- GitHub/Notion/Jira pattern comparison
- WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility guidelines

    "unit": "npm run test",
    "build": "npm run build -w @weekly-commit/shared && npm run build -w frontend && cd backend && ./gradlew build -x test",
    "e2e": "npx playwright test --config=e2e/playwright.config.ts",

    "generate:api-types": "./scripts/generate-api-types.sh",

content.text:
The plan is mostly solid and maps well to the decision doc. I only found one real blocker.

Non-blocking suggestions:
- For the collapsible AI sections, make sure the content is lazy-mounted or otherwise prevented from auto-fetching while collapsed; otherwise “collapsed by default” won’t actually reduce AI noise or auto-run behavior.
- `MyWeek` uses `useCurrentPlan()` / `getOrCreatePlan()`, so the “no plan” empty state is probably not a common runtime path; the more important empty state is the empty commit list.
- `RiskSignalsPanel.tsx` and `ReconcileAssistPanel.tsx` appear to be currently unused surfaces, so changes there are lower priority than the live routes.

Verdict: changes_requested
Blocking issues:
- Decision 3 / step 8 targets `frontend/src/components/ai/ReconcileAssistPanel.tsx`, but the live reconcile AI prefill flow is actually implemented directly in `frontend/src/routes/Reconcile.tsx` via `useAutoReconcileAssist`, `aiSuggestedOutcomes`, and the local outcome state. As written, that step changes the wrong surface for the user-facing behavior. The plan should explicitly target `Reconcile.tsx` (and related reconcile UI) for the ghost/preview-state change, or add a prior step to wire `ReconcileAssistPanel` into the page before relying on it.

{ "goal": "Implement 6 UI/UX research-backed decisions from docs/ui-ux-decisions.md: (1) Semantic colors for status components with WCAG AA-validated palette, (2) Progressive disclosure on My Week page, (3) AI suggestion tiering to reduce fatigue, (4) Dual font stack (Inter + Geist Mono) via host-token pipeline, (5) Charts library migration to Tremor/Recharts, (6) Loading & empty state improvements. Changes are scoped to the frontend package and the shared DesignTokens contract.", "steps": [ { "id": "step-1", "title": "Add WCAG AA-validated semantic color tokens to CSS theme", "description": "Decision 1, Part 1: Define semantic color tokens in `frontend/src/index.css`. The gap analysis found that several proposed palette values fail WCAG AA contrast for normal text (4.5:1 minimum). Before implementing, use corrected values that pass contrast checks: **Light mode (on white-ish backgrounds):** - Success base: `#3D7A4A` (was `#5F7A66`, boosted saturation/darkness for ≥4.5:1 on `#EEF3EF`) - Warning base: `#7A6520` (was `#8A7A5A`, darkened for ≥4.5:1 on `#F5F2EA`) - Danger base: `#9B3B3B` (was `#8A5C5C`, increased contrast for ≥4.5:1 on `#F6EEEE`) - Info/AI base: `#4A5578` (was `#5F6B85`, darkened for ≥4.5:1 on `#EEF1F6`) **Dark mode (on dark backgrounds):** - Success base: `#8FBF9A` (was `#7E9B87`, lightened for ≥4.5:1 on `#1A221C`) - Warning base: `#C4AC6A` (was `#A39473`, lightened for ≥4.5:1 on `#211F18`) - Danger base: `#D4A0A0` (was `#A37878`, lightened for ≥4.5:1 on `#221A1A`) - Info/AI base: `#9DABC8` (was `#7C8AA6`, lightened for ≥4.5:1 on `#1A1D24`) Background and border values from the original plan are fine (they don't carry text). Add new CSS custom properties in the `@theme` block: - `--color-success`, `--color-success-bg`, `--color-success-border` - `--color-warning`, `--color-warning-bg`, `--color-warning-border` - `--color-danger`, `--color-danger-bg`, `--color-danger-border` - `--color-info`, `--color-info-bg`, `--color-info-border` Update the existing `--color-success`, `--color-warning`, `--color-danger` values (which currently are gray `#404040`/`#737373`/`#171717` in light, vivid in dark) to the new muted semantic values. Add new `-bg` and `-border` tokens. Also update the `[data-theme="dark"]` block accordingly. Keep `--color-success-foreground`, `--color-warning-foreground`, `--color-danger-foreground` valid for button use (ensure they contrast against the base colors). Update `MockHostProvider.tsx` `defaultDesignTokens` to match the new light-mode success/warning/danger values. Update `AppShell.tsx` `tokensToStyle` to pass through the new token values. Note: The `DesignTokens` interface in `packages/shared/src/host.ts` already has `colorSuccess`, `colorWarning`, `colorDanger` — these continue to work. The new `-bg` and `-border` tokens are CSS-only (not part of the host contract) since they are implementation details of the module's internal design system.", "requiredChecks": ["lint", "typecheck", "build"] }, { "id": "step-2", "title": "Update Badge, Toast, and Button components for semantic colors", "description": "Decision 1, Part 2: Update status components to use the new semantic color tokens instead of monochrome foreground-based styling. **Badge.tsx** — Replace the `success`, `warning`, `danger` variant classes: - `success`: `border-success-border bg-success-bg text-success` (was `border-foreground/25 bg-foreground/5 text-foreground`) - `warning`: `border-warning-border bg-warning-bg text-warning` (was `border-foreground/15 bg-foreground/[0.03] text-muted`) - `danger`: `border-danger-border bg-danger-bg text-danger font-bold` (was `border-foreground/30 bg-foreground/8 text-foreground font-bold`) - Add new `info` variant: `border-info-border bg-info-bg text-info` Keep plan-state badge variants (`draft`, `locked`, `reconciling`, `reconciled`) monochrome. **Toast.tsx** — Update `toastVariants` to use semantic tokens: - `success`: `border-success-border bg-success-bg text-success` - `warning`: `border-warning-border bg-warning-bg text-warning` - `error`: `border-danger-border bg-danger-bg text-danger` - `info`: `border-info-border bg-info-bg text-info` **Button.tsx** — Update `danger` and `success` button variants to use the new semantic base colors. The `danger` variant: `bg-danger text-white` (ensure white contrasts against the new danger base). The `success` variant: `bg-success text-white`. Register the new color tokens in `@theme` so Tailwind utilities like `text-success`, `bg-success-bg`, `border-success-border` etc. resolve properly. Update `frontend/src/__tests__/ui/Badge.test.tsx` assertions — tests currently check for `text-foreground` and `text-muted` on semantic variants; update to check for `text-success`, `text-warning`, `text-danger` respectively.", "requiredChecks": ["lint", "typecheck", "unit", "build"] }, { "id": "step-3", "title": "Apply semantic colors to status indicators across the app", "description": "Decision 1, Part 3: Update all status-indicating components to use semantic colors with icon + text label redundancy. **MyWeek.tsx — ComplianceBadge**: Already uses `text-success`/`text-warning` — will now resolve to the new muted semantic colors automatically. Verify the ✓/⚠ icon + text pattern is preserved. **MyWeek.tsx — PlanStateBadge**: Keep monochrome (plan states are not severity indicators). **Reports.tsx — ExceptionAgingTable**: Update `SEVERITY_STYLES` map: - `CRITICAL`: use danger semantic tokens - `HIGH`: use warning semantic tokens - `MEDIUM`: keep neutral - `LOW`: keep neutral Update the STALE/AGING/NEW status labels to use `text-danger`/`text-warning`/`text-muted` (already correct). **Reports.tsx — VelocityTrendChart**: Trend indicator already uses `text-success`/`text-danger`. **Reports.tsx — ComplianceChart**: Update Shield icon to use `text-success` (already correct). **InsightPanel.tsx — severityBadgeVariant**: Already maps HIGH→danger, MEDIUM→warning — now those Badge variants will render with semantic colors. **ProactiveRiskBanner.tsx**: Update `SIGNAL_CONFIG` classes from `border-neutral-400 bg-neutral-100` to use danger/warning semantic background tokens for severity differentiation. **RiskSignalsPanel.tsx**: Update `SIGNAL_CLS` from neutral grays to semantic tokens based on signal severity. **ScopeChangeDialog.tsx**: Already uses `text-success`/`text-danger` for action type labels. **ReconcileAssistPanel.tsx**: Update `OUTCOME_COLORS` to use semantic tokens for ACHIEVED (success), NOT_ACHIEVED (danger), PARTIALLY_ACHIEVED (warning). **TicketDetailView.tsx**: Already maps outcomes to `text-success`/`text-warning`/`text-danger`. Ensure every status color use has icon + text label for accessibility redundancy (not color alone).", "requiredChecks": ["lint", "typecheck", "build"] }, { "id": "step-4", "title": "Install Inter font and update font token pipeline for dual font stack", "description": "Decision 4, Part 1: Switch the base font from monospace to proportional sans-serif. The font pipeline flows through 3 layers: (1) `packages/shared/src/host.ts` DesignTokens contract, (2) `frontend/src/host/MockHostProvider.tsx` default values, (3) `frontend/src/components/AppShell.tsx` which sets `--font-family-base` as an inline CSS var, and (4) `frontend/src/index.css` which uses it in `@theme` and `body`. **Changes:** 1. **`frontend/src/index.css`**: Add Google Fonts import for Inter alongside Geist Mono: `@import url("https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@300..900&family=Geist+Mono:wght@300..900&display=swap");` Update `@theme` `--font-family-base` default to: `var(--font-family-base, "Inter", "Geist Sans", system-ui, sans-serif)` Keep `--font-family-mono` as-is. 2. **`frontend/src/host/MockHostProvider.tsx`**: Update `defaultDesignTokens.fontFamilyBase` from the monospace string to `"'Inter', 'Geist Sans', system-ui, sans-serif"`. 3. **`frontend/src/components/AppShell.tsx`**: No structural changes needed — `tokensToStyle` already passes `tokens.fontFamilyBase` through to `--font-family-base`. The inline `fontFamily: 'var(--font-family-base)'` on the root div continues to work. 4. **Note on `packages/shared/src/host.ts`**: The `DesignTokens.fontFamilyBase` field is a string — no type change needed. The contract is that the host provides a font family string; we're just changing the default. This step only changes the default font. The next step handles adding `font-mono` classes to preserve monospace where needed.", "requiredChecks": ["lint", "typecheck", "build"] }, { "id": "step-5", "title": "Add font-mono utility classes to code-like content", "description": "Decision 4, Part 2: Now that the base font is proportional, explicitly apply `font-mono` (which maps to `--font-family-mono` = Geist Mono) to code-like content that should remain monospace. Audit and update these components: **Already using `font-mono`** (no change needed): - `TicketListView.tsx` — ticket keys - `TicketDetailView.tsx` — ticket keys - `UncommittedWorkSection.tsx` — ticket keys - `WhatIfPanel.tsx` — IDs **Need `font-mono` added:** - `CommitList.tsx` — commit titles (add `font-mono` to the title text span) - `CommitForm.tsx` — the title input field (add `font-mono` class) - `CapacityMeter.tsx` — estimate point values (the tally `{totalPoints} / {budgetPoints} pts`) - `Reports.tsx` — data cells in charts (point values, percentages, date labels marked as `text-[9px]` or `text-[10px]`) - `ScopeChangeTimeline.tsx` — commit titles in timeline events - `RcdoTreeView.tsx` — RCDO path identifiers - `AiLintPanel.tsx` — no change (prose suggestions should be sans-serif) - `Header.tsx` — week date display (date/time values) **Should NOT get font-mono** (keep proportional): - Navigation labels - Page headings (h1, h2) - Card titles - Button text - Body text / descriptions - Form labels - AI insight text - AI summary prose Update related tests if any assert on class names that would change.", "requiredChecks": ["lint", "typecheck", "unit", "build"] }, { "id": "step-6", "title": "Create CollapsibleSection shared component with localStorage persistence", "description": "Decision 2, Prerequisite: Build a reusable `CollapsibleSection` component before refactoring My Week. Create `frontend/src/components/shared/CollapsibleSection.tsx`: ```tsx interface CollapsibleSectionProps { id: string; // localStorage key suffix title: string; // Section heading defaultExpanded?: boolean; badge?: ReactNode; // e.g., "3 suggestions" count badge children: ReactNode; } ``` Features: - Renders a header row with title, optional badge, and chevron toggle - Persists expand/collapse state to `localStorage` under key `wc-section-{id}` - Supports `defaultExpanded` prop (used on first visit before localStorage has a value) - Accessible: uses `aria-expanded`, `aria-controls`, and `role="region"` on content - Styled consistently with the existing Card/CardHeader pattern (border-border, bg-surface) - Smooth height transition using CSS `grid-template-rows` trick (0fr → 1fr) Also create `frontend/src/components/shared/usePersistedState.ts` — a generic hook for localStorage-backed state used by CollapsibleSection and potentially other components. Write unit tests in `frontend/src/__tests__/CollapsibleSection.test.tsx`: - Renders title and children - Toggles open/close on click - Respects defaultExpanded prop - Persists state to localStorage - Renders badge when provided", "requiredChecks": ["lint", "typecheck", "unit", "build"] }, { "id": "step-7", "title": "Implement progressive disclosure on My Week page", "description": "Decision 2: Refactor `frontend/src/routes/MyWeek.tsx` to wrap sections in CollapsibleSection and add conditional visibility. **Always visible** (no change): - Plan header with state badge + action buttons - Capacity meter - Commit list - Week selector + page header **Contextually visible** (add conditional guards — some already exist): - Risk banners: already guarded by `isLocked` ✓ - Auto-lock system banner: already guarded by `plan?.systemLockedWithErrors` ✓ - Scope change timeline: already guarded by `isLocked && scopeChangeEvents.length > 0` ✓ - Pre-lock validation: already guarded by `isDraft && showPreLockValidation` ✓ **Wrap in CollapsibleSection** (with defaults): - AI Lint Panel: `...}>`. Extract lint result count to show as badge summary (e.g., "3 hints"). - AI Insights (InsightPanel): `` - What-If Planner: Already collapsed via its own UI ✓ — but wrap in CollapsibleSection for consistency - Plan History: Already has show/hide toggle — replace with CollapsibleSection for consistency - Carry-forward Lineage: Keep as-is (it's triggered by specific commit action, not always present) - Soft Warnings Panel: `` where `shouldExpandWarnings` is true when commits > 8 or pawn ratio > 40% **Add "Expand all / Collapse all" toggle** at the top of the page for power users. Implement as a button that programmatically sets all section localStorage keys. Preserve all existing `data-testid` attributes and ensure existing tests still pass.", "requiredChecks": ["lint", "typecheck", "unit", "build"] }, { "id": "step-8", "title": "Implement AI suggestion tiering and dismiss memory", "description": "Decision 3: Adjust AI component default states and add dismiss memory. **Changes to AI components:** 1. **AiLintPanel** (already addressed in step-7 via CollapsibleSection with collapsed default + badge summary). No additional changes needed beyond the collapse wrapper. 2. **InsightPanel** on My Week: Already addressed in step-7 (collapsed by default with CollapsibleSection). 3. **ManagerAiSummaryCard** on Team Week (`frontend/src/routes/TeamWeek.tsx`): Wrap in CollapsibleSection with `id="manager-ai-summary"` and `defaultExpanded={false}`. Show the first line of `summaryText` as a preview in the section header badge. 4. **ProactiveRiskBanner** on My Week: Add a count badge approach — show a summary line "⚠ N risk signals detected" that expands to full banners on click. Wrap in CollapsibleSection with `id="risk-banners"` and `defaultExpanded={true}` (critical signals should default open but can be collapsed). 5. **Reconcile page AI prefill** (`frontend/src/routes/Reconcile.tsx`): The live AI prefill flow is implemented directly in `Reconcile.tsx` via `useAutoReconcileAssist`, `aiSuggestedOutcomes` state, and a `useEffect` that auto-applies outcomes into `localOutcomes`. Change this from auto-apply to ghost/preview state: - Instead of immediately setting `localOutcomes` with AI values in the `useEffect`, store AI suggestions only in `aiSuggestedOutcomes` (do NOT merge into `localOutcomes` until user confirms). - In `ReconcileCommitRow`, when a commit has an entry in `aiSuggestedOutcomes` but NOT in `localOutcomes`, render the `OutcomeSelector` in a ghost/preview visual state: faded text with `bg-info-bg` background, the `AiSuggestedBadge` prominently shown, and a "Click to accept" affordance. - When the user clicks the ghost outcome or explicitly selects any outcome, move it from `aiSuggestedOutcomes` into `localOutcomes` (solidifying the selection). - Similarly for AI-prefilled notes: show them as placeholder/ghost text in the textarea (using `placeholder` or a faded overlay) rather than pre-filling `localNotes`. - The `ReconcileAssistPanel` component (`frontend/src/components/ai/ReconcileAssistPanel.tsx`) is a separate manual-trigger panel not currently used by the Reconcile page — leave it unchanged in this step. 6. **TeamWeek InsightPanel**: Already has a toggle (`insightsExpanded`). Change default to collapsed: `const [insightsExpanded, setInsightsExpanded] = useState(false);` **Dismiss memory system:** Create `frontend/src/lib/useDismissMemory.ts` hook: - Tracks dismiss count per component ID in localStorage - `useDismissMemory(componentId: string, threshold?: number)` returns `{ shouldAutoCollapse: boolean, recordDismiss: () => void }` - Default threshold: 3 consecutive dismissals → auto-collapse - Wire into AiLintPanel and InsightPanel CollapsibleSection defaults **Global AI preference:** Add to the existing feature flags or create a new localStorage-based setting `wc-ai-mode` with values `'full'` | `'on-demand'`. When `'on-demand'`, all AI sections default to collapsed and auto-run is disabled. Add a small toggle in the sidebar footer or header settings area.", "requiredChecks": ["lint", "typecheck", "unit", "build"] }, { "id": "step-9", "title": "Create skeleton loading variants for major page surfaces", "description": "Decision 6, Part 1: Replace text-only loading states with skeleton loaders. The existing `Skeleton` component in `frontend/src/components/ui/Skeleton.tsx` is a simple animated div. Build composed skeleton variants: **Create `frontend/src/components/shared/skeletons/`:** 1. **`PlanHeaderSkeleton.tsx`**: Mimics the plan header Card layout — badge placeholder, action button placeholders. 2. **`CommitListSkeleton.tsx`**: Shows 3-4 skeleton commit rows matching the `CommitList` SortableItem layout — grip handle, title bar, chess piece dot, points, action buttons. 3. **`TeamWeekSkeleton.tsx`**: Overview card grid with skeleton stat boxes matching `OverviewSection`. 4. **`ReportChartSkeleton.tsx`**: Generic chart placeholder — card with header skeleton + rectangular area for chart body. **Update routes to use skeletons:** - `MyWeek.tsx`: Replace `
Loading plan…
` with `` + `` - `TeamWeek.tsx`: Replace `
Loading team data…
` with `` - `Reports.tsx`: Replace `

Loading report data…

` with grid of `` - `Rcdos.tsx`: Replace `Loading…` text with a tree-like skeleton - `Tickets.tsx`: Replace loading state with table skeleton rows **Tier 2 — AI loading states** (already partially implemented): - `InsightPanel` already has `InsightSkeletons` ✓ - `ManagerAiSummaryCard` already has `ManagerAiSummarySkeleton` ✓ - `AiLintPanel`: Update "Analyzing commit quality…" to include shimmer animation **Tier 3 — Timeout fallback:** Create `frontend/src/components/shared/LoadingWithTimeout.tsx`: - Shows skeleton for first N seconds - After timeout (default 5s), shows "Still loading… [Retry]" with elapsed time - Takes `onRetry` callback prop", "requiredChecks": ["lint", "typecheck", "unit", "build"] }, { "id": "step-10", "title": "Add empty state components for major surfaces", "description": "Decision 6, Part 2: Create rich empty states with description + CTA for each major surface. Create `frontend/src/components/shared/EmptyState.tsx`: ```tsx interface EmptyStateProps { icon?: ReactNode; // Lucide icon or emoji illustration title: string; // e.g., "No commits yet" description?: string; // Explanatory text action?: ReactNode; // CTA button 'data-testid'?: string; } ``` Styled consistently: centered content, muted text, subtle border, generous padding. **Apply to surfaces:** 1. **MyWeek (no plan / empty commits)**: `planLoading` is false and `!plan` → show EmptyState with title "Start your week", description "Add your first commitment to begin planning", CTA → Add Commit button. 2. **CommitList (empty)**: When `commits.length === 0` and plan exists → EmptyState "No commits yet" with prominent Add button. Currently shows nothing. 3. **TeamWeek (no team selected)**: Already has a "No team selected" div — enhance with EmptyState component for consistency. 4. **Reports (no data)**: When all report arrays are empty → EmptyState "Not enough data yet — reports will populate after your first reconciled week". 5. **RCDO tree (empty)**: When `tree` is empty array → EmptyState "No strategy nodes — create your first Rally Cry to get started" with CTA if user has permissions. 6. **Tickets (empty results)**: When ticket list returns 0 results → EmptyState "No tickets match your filters" or "No tickets yet — create your first ticket". Write unit test for EmptyState component.", "requiredChecks": ["lint", "typecheck", "unit", "build"] }, { "id": "step-11", "title": "Install Tremor charting library and migrate velocity trend chart", "description": "Decision 5, Part 1: Add Tremor as a dependency and migrate the first (most complex) chart as proof of concept. **Install:** `npm install @tremor/react` in the frontend package. Tremor is Tailwind-native and works with Tailwind v4. **Migrate `VelocityTrendChart`** in `frontend/src/routes/Reports.tsx`: - Replace the manual CSS bar chart + rolling average dots with Tremor's `AreaChart` or `BarChart` component - Include the rolling 4-week average as a second series (line overlay) - Preserve the trend indicator badge in the header - Add proper tooltips (Tremor provides them out of the box) - Ensure dark mode works (Tremor respects Tailwind dark mode) - Keep the same Card wrapper and header layout **Keep pure CSS for:** - `CapacityMeter` progress bar (simple, works well) - Chess distribution stacked bar (unique visual, Tremor doesn't have a great match) Verify bundle size impact is acceptable. If Tremor has issues with Tailwind v4 compatibility, fall back to Recharts (`npm install recharts`) which has no CSS framework dependency. Update any snapshot or assertion tests for Reports if they exist.", "requiredChecks": ["lint", "typecheck", "build"] }, { "id": "step-12", "title": "Migrate remaining charts to charting library", "description": "Decision 5, Part 2: Migrate the remaining CSS charts to the charting library chosen in step-11. **Charts to migrate:** 1. **PlannedVsAchievedChart**: Grouped bar chart (planned vs achieved per week). Use `BarChart` with two series. 2. **AchievementRateChart**: Bar chart with target line at 80%. Use `BarChart` with reference line. 3. **CarryForwardChart**: Horizontal bar chart showing carry-forward rates. Use `BarChart` horizontal or `BarList`. 4. **ComplianceChart**: Multi-series horizontal bars (lock rate, reconcile rate, auto-lock). Use grouped `BarChart`. 5. **ScopeChangeChart**: Vertical bars with HIGH annotation. Use `BarChart` with conditional coloring. **Keep as CSS:** - Chess distribution stacked bar (custom visual) - Exception aging table (it's a table, not a chart) - AI acceptance card bars (simple progress bars) Each migrated chart should: - Maintain the same Card wrapper and title - Use consistent color tokens (integrate with our semantic color system where appropriate) - Include proper ARIA labels and screen reader support - Handle empty data states gracefully - Support dark mode This is an incremental migration — each chart is a self-contained function component in Reports.tsx.", "requiredChecks": ["lint", "typecheck", "build"] } ] }

content.text:
The revised plan addresses the previous blocker: step 8 now correctly targets the live AI prefill flow in `frontend/src/routes/Reconcile.tsx` and explicitly leaves the unused `ReconcileAssistPanel.tsx` out of that change.

I don’t see any remaining issues that make the plan impossible or dangerous to implement. The file targets and sequencing are workable.

Non-blocking suggestions:
- For collapsed AI sections, consider lazy-mounting content so “collapsed by default” also suppresses fetch/auto-run behavior where appropriate.
- For the My Week empty state, the more meaningful case is likely “plan exists but has no commits,” since `useCurrentPlan()` uses get-or-create behavior.
- For chart migration, deciding Tremor vs Recharts up front during implementation may reduce churn, but the fallback note is acceptable as written.

Verdict: approve
